elements.title: the tallest onshore wind turbine in England
elements.contents: ... that the tallest onshore wind turbine in England (pictured) is owned by the community of Lawrence Weston?

elements.title: a pulp mill
elements.contents: ... that a pulp mill was fined by the state of Washington for its greenhouse gas emissions, even though it had been closed for the past two years?

elements.title: castle of Smar Jbeil
elements.contents: ... that the Crusader castle of Smar Jbeil was built atop a Roman-period necropolis?

elements.title: Bubo Barnett
elements.contents: ... that "tank-resembling" Bubo Barnett was "unstopped by anything but a pastry window"?

elements.title: a New York City office tower
elements.contents: ... that the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that plans for a New York City office tower could be rejected?

elements.title: William Bramwell Smith Jr.
elements.contents: ... that William Bramwell Smith Jr. composed music for the presidential inauguration of John F. Kennedy?

elements.title: Little Sir Echo
elements.contents: ... that the camp song "Little Sir Echo" has been recorded in big-band, punk, country, and French versions, and from performances by Jerry Lewis and the Wiggles?

elements.title: an Idaho TV station
elements.contents: ... that an Idaho TV station warned businesses, "The rumors of our going dark are not unfounded"?

elements.title: Henry Patenson
elements.contents: ... that Henry Patenson threw rocks at aggravating courtiers while at the Holy Roman Emperor's court?
